items.prompt: What should guidelines include for each label class?
choices: Definitions, edge cases, and positive and negative examples
items.explain: Exemplars and edge-case rules reduce forced guesses and drift.

items.prompt: When should teams re-audit random batches?
choices: After guideline or tool changes and on a ongoing drift schedule
items.explain: Schema or tool changes and long projects both require drift detection.

items.prompt: Pre-labeling should propose candidates for human review until audit error rates meet threshold—not replace review by default.
items.explain: Efficiency gains must not trade away accuracy or fairness.

items.prompt: Name two practices from the diversity subsection for labels that encode social constructs.
items.rubric: Diverse reviewers, subgroup agreement tests, fixed-cadence slice audits.
items.sample_answer: Include diverse reviewers and test subgroup agreement while auditing labeled slices on a fixed cadence.

items.prompt: Why version the guideline document when schemas change?
choices: So annotators can be retrained on diffs and decisions stay comparable
items.explain: Versioning supports retraining and comparable labels over time.
